independent_variables.variable_description: Whether municipal government is aligned with the federal incumbent president/party
Indicators of urbanization, education, and socioeconomic development at the municipal level
Degree of party fragmentation and political competition in the municipality
Coverage of social programs (e.g., Bolsa Família) and human development indicators (IDHM)

dependent_variables.variable_description: Vote-revealed ideological position of the municipality, constructed from proportional representation election results weighted by party ideology scores from the Brazilian Legislative Survey

brief_justification: This is an empirical quantitative study that constructs a municipal-level ideology index from proportional representation election results in Brazil (1994-2018) and tests four sets of explanatory factors using regression analysis. The paper uses a model-based approach (kitchen sink linear model) without an explicit identification strategy or stated identification assumptions. It makes implicit causal claims by framing incumbency alignment as having a 'gravitational effect' on local ideology, though it does not use explicit causal language such as 'causes' or 'causal effect.'
